title: Excluded Websites Blocklist — Prevent AI Access to Financial Institutions
close_reason: The manual domain whitelist / restricted mode already solves this. Users must explicitly approve domains before AI can interact with them, which prevents unauthorized access to financial institutions by default. No separate blocklist needed.

title: Pairing-based auth for MCP agent access
description:
## Problem
The MCP server endpoints are completely unauthenticated. Any process on localhost can control the browser via MCP. The current setup also requires manual copy/paste of a vf:// connection string which is high friction.

## Solution
Implement a one-time pairing flow where AI agents request access, the human approves in the Chrome extension popup, and the agent receives a persistent API key.

## Auth Flow

1. AI agent connects to MCP server and calls any tool
2. Server rejects with an error message:
   - 'Authentication required. Include your API key as the X-API-Key header or apiKey query parameter in requests. If you do not have a key, call the request_pairing tool to initiate pairing. If you have previously paired, check your working directory for a webpilot.key file.'
3. AI agent either:
   a. Finds an existing webpilot.key file in its working directory and includes the key in subsequent requests
   b. Calls the request_pairing MCP tool to start pairing
4. request_pairing sends a pairing request to the Chrome extension via WebSocket, including a human-readable agent name/identifier
5. Chrome extension popup shows the pairing request with Approve / Deny buttons
6. On APPROVE: server generates a unique API key for this agent, returns it to the AI in the tool response, tells the AI to save it to webpilot.key in its working directory
7. On DENY: server returns a rejection message to the AI
8. AI saves the key to webpilot.key and includes it in all subsequent MCP requests

## Key Management
- One unique key per paired agent
- Keys are stored server-side in the config directory (e.g., <dataDir>/config/paired-keys.json)
- Keys are revocable from the Chrome extension popup UI
- Extension popup shows a list of paired agents with revoke buttons
- Revoking a key immediately invalidates it -- subsequent requests with that key fail with the auth error

## MCP Changes
- All existing MCP tool calls (except request_pairing) require a valid API key
- request_pairing is the only unauthenticated endpoint
- Auth key can be provided via X-API-Key header or apiKey query parameter on /sse and /message endpoints
- Error responses must be descriptive enough for an AI agent to self-recover (find key file or initiate pairing)

## Chrome Extension Changes
- Popup UI: new section showing pairing requests (approve/deny)
- Popup UI: new section showing paired agents list with revoke buttons
- WebSocket message types: pairing_request, pairing_response from server
- Store paired agent metadata (name, paired date) for display

## Server Changes
- Auth middleware on MCP endpoints (all except request_pairing)
- New request_pairing tool registration in MCP handler
- Pairing request/response WebSocket protocol with extension
- Key generation (crypto.randomUUID or similar)
- Key storage and validation in paired-keys.json
- Key revocation support via extension WebSocket message

## Out of scope
- Removing the existing vf:// connection string flow (keep as fallback)
- Key rotation or expiration (just revocation for now)
- Multi-machine or network auth (localhost only)
- Changes to the WebSocket auth between server and extension (that stays as-is)

## Expected behavior
- Fresh AI agent hits MCP -> gets clear auth error with instructions -> calls request_pairing -> human approves in popup -> agent gets key -> saves to webpilot.key -> all subsequent sessions auto-authenticate
- Revoking a key in the popup immediately blocks that agent
- Multiple agents can each have their own key

title: Dynamic Platform Optimizer Architecture -- Client-Loadable, AI-Improvable Optimizers
description:
## Summary

Rearchitect the platform optimizer system so optimizers can be **loaded and updated by clients in real-time**, enabling AI agents to autonomously resolve issues with them and improve their functionality over time.

## Current State

Platform optimizers are static — bundled at build time and require code changes + releases to update.

## Proposed Architecture

### Dynamic Loading
- Optimizers should be loadable/swappable at runtime by clients
- Hot-reload support — no restart required when an optimizer is updated
- Version tracking so clients can roll back if an update causes regressions

### AI Agent Improvement Loop
- When an optimizer fails or produces poor results on a platform, AI agents should be able to:
  1. Diagnose what went wrong (element not found, selector stale, layout changed)
  2. Generate an improved optimizer or patch
  3. Test the fix against the target platform
  4. Deploy the updated optimizer to the client in real-time
- This creates a self-healing system where optimizers evolve with platform changes

### Element Finding / Selector Resilience
- Reference: [Scrapling](https://github.com/D4Vinci/Scrapling) — an adaptive web scraping framework
- Key ideas to borrow from Scrapling:
  - **Adaptive selectors** that survive DOM changes (not just CSS/XPath)
  - **Smart element matching** using multiple signals (text content, attributes, position, similarity)
  - **Automatic selector repair** when elements move or get renamed
  - **Stealth/anti-detection** patterns for reliable access
- The goal: optimizers should find elements reliably even when platforms update their UI

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Optimizers can be loaded/updated at runtime without restart
- [ ] API or interface for AI agents to submit optimizer improvements
- [ ] Versioning and rollback support for optimizers
- [ ] Element finding uses adaptive/resilient selectors inspired by Scrapling
- [ ] Test harness for validating optimizer changes before deployment
- [ ] Documentation for the optimizer authoring format

## References

- [Scrapling](https://github.com/D4Vinci/Scrapling) — adaptive element finding, smart selectors, anti-detection

title: New browser tabs open in unnamed/uncolored tab group instead of blue WebPilot group
description: When opening a new browser tab via the WebPilot MCP server (browser_create_tab), the tab is placed in an unnamed and uncolored tab group. Previously, tabs were placed in a blue-colored group named 'WebPilot'. This is a regression — the tab group naming and coloring needs to be restored.

comments.author: Jacob Tonna
comments.text: Approved plan: [Sub-task 1] Add await to addTabToGroup() at 3 call sites in handlers/tabs.js (~lines 162, 175, 185). Complexity: light. [Sub-task 2] Manual verification across all 3 code paths. Complexity: light. Depends on sub-task 1.

comments.author: Jacob Tonna
comments.text: Investigation complete. The await fix (commit 0302d41) is correct and resolves the race condition in our code. The remaining visual issue — tab group title/color not rendering until user interaction — is a confirmed Chrome 145 rendering bug. The API succeeds (verified via chrome.tabGroups.get() returning correct title and color), but Chrome does not repaint the tab group UI. This is fixed in Chrome 146. No further code changes needed on our side.

title: Request Chaining — Execute multiple tool calls sequentially and return combined results
close_reason: Implemented browser_request_chain MCP tool: sequential tool execution with result referencing, return modes, and pre-validation. Updated all documentation.

comments.author: Jacob Tonna
comments.text:
## Description

Add a new MCP tool called `browser_request_chain` that accepts an array of tool calls, executes them sequentially (one after another), and returns the combined results.

## Context

Currently, the MCP server (mcp-handler.js) handles one tool call per request. Clients that need to run a sequence of operations (e.g., create tab → get accessibility tree → click element → get updated tree) must make separate round-trips for each step.

Request chaining would allow a single MCP call to specify multiple operations in order, where each executes after the previous one completes. Results from earlier steps could potentially be referenced by later steps (e.g., tab_id from create_tab used in subsequent calls).

## Key files
- packages/server-for-chrome-extension/src/mcp-handler.js (tool definitions + handleToolCall)
- packages/server-for-chrome-extension/src/extension-bridge.js (command execution)
- packages/chrome-extension-unpacked/background.js (extension-side routing)

## Acceptance criteria
- New `browser_request_chain` tool registered in mcp-handler.js
- Accepts an array of {tool, arguments} objects
- Executes each tool call sequentially via existing handleToolCall logic
- Returns an array of results in execution order
- Stops execution and returns partial results + error if any step fails
- Results from prior steps can be referenced in later steps (e.g., \/usr/bin/bash.tab_id)

comments.author: Jacob Tonna
comments.text:
## Addendum: Return mode option

Add a parameter (e.g., `return_mode`) with two options:
- `"all"` — return results from every step in the chain (default)
- `"last"` — return only the final step's result

This keeps payloads small when the caller only cares about the end state (e.g., get the accessibility tree after a series of clicks), while still allowing full traceability when needed.

comments.author: Jacob Tonna
comments.text:
## Resolved decisions (for autonomous execution)

### Result referencing syntax
Simple dot-path only: `$N.path.to.value` where N is the zero-based step index.
- Example: `$0.tab_id` resolves to the tab_id field from step 0's result.
- No array indexing or bracket notation.
- If a reference cannot be resolved (missing key, invalid step index), the step fails immediately with a clear error before execution.
- References are string values in the arguments object. A value is treated as a reference only if it matches the pattern `$\d+\.` (dollar sign + digit(s) + dot + path).

### Max chain length
No limit. The caller can pass as many steps as needed.

### Error response shape
When a step fails, return:
```json
{
  "results": [/* results from completed steps */],
  "error": {
    "step": 2,
    "tool": "browser_click",
    "message": "Element not found: #missing-btn"
  }
}
```
When all steps succeed and return_mode is "all", return:
```json
{
  "results": [/* all step results */]
}
```
When return_mode is "last", return only the final step's result directly (no wrapper).

### Pre-validation
Before executing any step, validate that all tool names in the chain are registered tools. If any tool name is invalid, fail immediately with an error listing the invalid tool(s) — do not execute any steps.

comments.author: Jacob Tonna
comments.text:
## Implementation Plan

### Sub-task 1: Register tool definition [light]
- Add browser_request_chain to tools array in mcp-handler.js
- Schema: steps (required array of {tool, arguments}), return_mode (optional, default all)

### Sub-task 2: Implement resolveReferences helper [standard]
- Deep-walks arguments, resolves $N.path.to.value strings against parsed prior results
- JSON.parse from previousResults[N].content[0].text, catch parse errors
- Dot-path traversal, numeric keys for arrays, non-string values pass through

### Sub-task 3: Chain execution logic [standard]
- Add browser_request_chain case in handleToolCall with early return
- Pre-validate tool names + reference indices before execution
- Sequential loop: resolve refs -> execute -> store result
- Empty steps: {results:[]} for all mode, error for last mode
- Depends on sub-tasks 1 and 2

All changes in single file: mcp-handler.js (~120 lines added)

title: Unified version sync and v0.4.0 release
description:
## Problem
Version numbers are scattered across 5+ locations in the monorepo and have drifted out of sync:
- Root package.json: 0.3.0
- Electron package.json: 0.3.0
- Server package.json: 0.3.0
- Chrome extension manifest.json: 0.2.0 (outdated)
- MCP handler serverInfo in mcp-handler.js: 0.2.0 (hardcoded, outdated)
- Server package-lock.json: 0.2.0 (stale)
- Latest GitHub release: v0.3.2

## Scope
1. Create a version sync script (e.g. scripts/bump-version.js) that reads the version from root package.json and updates all other locations:
   - packages/electron/package.json (version field)
   - packages/server-for-chrome-extension/package.json (version field)
   - packages/chrome-extension-unpacked/manifest.json (version field)
   - packages/server-for-chrome-extension/src/mcp-handler.js (serverInfo.version)
   - packages/server-for-chrome-extension/package-lock.json (version field at top level)
2. Add an npm script in root package.json: "version:sync" that runs the script
3. Bump all versions to 0.4.0 using the new script
4. Create a GitHub release v0.4.0 from main after merging

## Expected behavior
- Running 'npm run version:sync' reads root package.json version and updates all 5 other locations in lockstep
- All version references in the codebase match after running the script
- v0.4.0 release exists on GitHub with the correct tag

## Out of scope
- CI/CD pipeline changes
- Semantic-release or changeset adoption
- npm publish workflows
- Auto-merge (leave PR open for human review)
close_reason: Implemented version sync script (scripts/bump-version.js) and npm run version:sync. Bumped all 7 version locations to 0.4.0. Updated docs to remove stale known issue and version references.

comments.author: Jacob Tonna
comments.text: Plan approved (autonomous). Sub-tasks: (1) Create scripts/bump-version.js [standard], (2) Add version:sync npm script [light], (3) Run bump to 0.4.0 [light], (4) GitHub release in PR phase [light].

title: Restricted mode: domain whitelist with default-block-all
description: EMERGENCY SECURITY FEATURE. Add a restricted mode toggle (ON by default) that blocks ALL WebPilot MCP commands on any site not in a user-managed whitelist. Scope: (1) Extension-level block — any MCP command involving a tab is rejected if the tab domain is not whitelisted. (2) Rejection message returned to AI agent: 'Blocked: [domain] is not whitelisted. The human must manually add this site to the whitelist in the WebPilot extension before automation can proceed.' (3) Domain-level granularity — e.g. yahoo.com covers all subdomains and paths. (4) UI in extension popup: restricted mode ON/OFF toggle, button to whitelist/remove current site, editable list of all whitelisted domains. (5) Storage in chrome.storage.local. (6) Default state: ON — all sites blocked until manually whitelisted. (7) Independent of blocklist ticket WebPilot-03d — both coexist.

comments.author: Jacob Tonna
comments.text: Approved plan (3 sub-tasks): [Sub-task 1] STANDARD — Add whitelist check gate in background.js handleServerCommand(). Gate ALL commands except get_tabs. Cache whitelist in memory via storage.onChanged. Fail-closed: block if chrome.tabs.get() throws or URL has no valid hostname. [Sub-task 2] STANDARD — Add popup UI: restricted mode toggle (default ON), one-click whitelist/remove current site button, domain input + add button, editable domain list with remove buttons. Normalize input. [Sub-task 3] LIGHT — Copy modified files to electron/dist and dist directories. Depends on 1+2.

title: Implement onboarding wizard in Electron app
WebPilot window mode creates small window — should remember last size/position
description: When tabMode is set to window, chrome.windows.create() in createTab() does not pass width/height/top/left parameters, so Chrome uses a small default window size. Resizing the window has no effect — next time a new WebPilot window is created it reverts to the small default. Fix: persist the window bounds (width, height, top, left) to chrome.storage.local whenever the user resizes/moves the WebPilot window, and restore those bounds when creating a new window.

comments.author: Jacob Tonna
comments.text: Approved plan: [Sub-task 1] STANDARD — Add chrome.windows.onBoundsChanged listener in background.js to save WebPilot window bounds to chrome.storage.local. Modify createTab() in tabs.js to load saved bounds and pass to chrome.windows.create(). Add windows permission to manifest.json. [Sub-task 2] LIGHT — Sync dist copies. Run docs scout.
